title: eriophorum intercedens var. rufescens
intro: Eriophorum intercedens var. rufescens, also known as the red cotton grass or woolly cotton grass, is a perennial plant species within the Eriophorum genus, characterized by its distinctive, cotton-like seeds and reddish-brown coloration. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eriophorum intercedens var. rufescens include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce congestion, and promote relaxation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where the roots and rhizomes are typically harvested for their medicinal compounds.
